🐳 DockCheck — Analysis Report

Generated on 2026-04-12 15:40:16

Summary

20
Total Issues
6
Critical
10
Medium
4
Low

Metadata

swarm_path tests\fixtures\docker-stack.bad.yml

Issues

CRITICAL (6)

SW-002 critical
Component: swarm/web
Service has no CPU or memory limits defined (resources.limits)
SW-004 critical
Component: swarm/web
Service has no restart policy defined or no bounded max_attempts
SW-007 critical
Component: swarm/web
Service passes sensitive data (passwords, secrets, API keys) via environment variables instead of Docker Swarm secrets
SW-002 critical
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no CPU or memory limits defined (resources.limits)
SW-004 critical
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no restart policy defined or no bounded max_attempts
SW-007 critical
Component: swarm/worker
Service passes sensitive data (passwords, secrets, API keys) via environment variables instead of Docker Swarm secrets

MEDIUM (10)

SW-001 medium
Component: swarm/web
Service has no explicit replica count defined in deploy configuration
SW-006 medium
Component: swarm/web
Service has no rolling update configuration (update_config)
SW-008 medium
Component: swarm/web
Service uses ':latest' tag or unversioned image, undermining deployment reproducibility
SW-009 medium
Component: swarm/web
Service relies on the default network instead of an explicitly defined overlay network
SW-010 medium
Component: swarm/web
Service has no health check defined, preventing Swarm from detecting and replacing unhealthy tasks
SW-001 medium
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no explicit replica count defined in deploy configuration
SW-006 medium
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no rolling update configuration (update_config)
SW-008 medium
Component: swarm/worker
Service uses ':latest' tag or unversioned image, undermining deployment reproducibility
SW-009 medium
Component: swarm/worker
Service relies on the default network instead of an explicitly defined overlay network
SW-010 medium
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no health check defined, preventing Swarm from detecting and replacing unhealthy tasks

LOW (4)

SW-003 low
Component: swarm/web
Service has no CPU or memory reservations defined (resources.reservations)
SW-005 low
Component: swarm/web
Service has no placement constraints defined
SW-003 low
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no CPU or memory reservations defined (resources.reservations)
SW-005 low
Component: swarm/worker
Service has no placement constraints defined

Recommendations

SW-001 medium

Define an explicit 'replicas' value under 'deploy' to control service scaling and avoid ambiguity.

SW-002 critical

Define 'resources.limits' with CPU and memory caps to prevent a single service from monopolising cluster resources.

SW-003 low

Define 'resources.reservations' to guarantee a minimum allocation and improve scheduling predictability.

SW-004 critical

Define a 'restart_policy' with 'condition', 'delay', and bounded 'max_attempts' to avoid infinite restart loops destabilising nodes.

SW-005 low

Use 'placement.constraints' to control service scheduling across nodes (e.g., pin database services to specific nodes).

SW-006 medium

Define 'update_config' with 'parallelism' and 'delay' to ensure zero-downtime deployments during rolling updates.

SW-007 critical

Use the Docker Swarm secrets mechanism instead of environment variables for sensitive data. Env vars may be visible in logs or 'docker inspect' output.

SW-008 medium

Pin service images to a specific version tag or digest to guarantee reproducible deployments across all Swarm nodes.

SW-009 medium

Attach services to explicitly defined overlay networks to enforce proper service isolation and avoid unintended cross-service communication.

SW-010 medium

Define a 'healthcheck' with 'test', 'interval', 'timeout', and 'retries' to allow Swarm's scheduler to detect and replace unhealthy tasks.

Performance

duration_s 0.0107
DockCheck — Static analysis tool for Docker environments
TFE — EPHEC | Thomas Girboux | 2026
